payload.text:
Role

당신은 세계적인 수준의 UX/UI 디자이너이자 숙련된 React 프론트엔드 개발자입니다.

Project Goal

'여수 아이디어 공모전'을 위한 모바일 웹 앱 프로토타입 **'YE:ON (여수:연)'**을 만들어주세요.
이 앱은 관광객이 여수를 여행할 때 '디지털 수군(Navy)'이 되어 미션을 수행하고, 기록을 남기는 게이미피케이션 관광 플랫폼입니다.

Tech Stack

Framework: React (Single Page Application)

Styling: Tailwind CSS (모바일 퍼스트 디자인 필수)

Icons: Lucide-React

Components: shadcn/ui 스타일의 깔끔하고 현대적인 컴포넌트

Design Concept & Tone

테마: "디지털 전라좌수영" (Digital Jeolla Left Navy)

컬러 팔레트:

Primary: 깊은 바다색 (Deep Ocean Blue, #0F172A)

Accent: 신호연의 오방색을 현대적으로 재해석한 Red(#EF4444), Yellow(#EAB308)

Background: 깔끔한 화이트/라이트 그레이

분위기: 한국적인 전통미(호패, 신호연)와 현대적인 IT 기술(블록체인, AR)이 융합된 세련된 느낌.

Key Features & Pages (Implementation Detail)

앱은 하단 네비게이션 바(Bottom Tab)를 통해 다음 4가지 핵심 탭으로 구성됩니다.

1. 홈 (디지털 호패 & 대시보드)

디지털 호패 (ID Card): - 화면 중앙에 '디지털 시민증' 카드가 있어야 함.

사용자 이름(예: 홍길동), 계급(예: 명예 수군), 소속(여수 전라좌수영), QR 코드가 포함된 디자인.

카드는 전통적인 나무 질감 느낌이나 골드 테두리로 고급스럽게 표현.

상태 요약: 현재 보유한 '군량미(포인트)'와 '수행 완료한 미션 수' 표시.

워치 연동 버튼: "워치 페어링 중..." 이라는 상태 표시와 함께 햅틱 아이콘 표시.

2. AR 연 뷰어 (Map & Mission)

지도 인터페이스: (실제 지도가 어렵다면) 여수 주요 관광지(진남관, 이순신광장, 오동도)가 표시된 스타일리시한 약도 이미지나 Mock Map UI 구현.

신호연 마커: 지도 위에 '전술 신호연(Kite)' 아이콘이 떠 있고, 클릭하면 미션 팝업이 뜸.

예: "이순신 광장에서 거북선을 찾으시오."

AR 모드 버튼: 화면 상단에 [AR 카메라 켜기] 버튼 배치 (클릭 시 카메라 뷰 오버레이 시뮬레이션).

3. 기록관 (AI 난중일기)

타임라인 UI: 사용자의 이동 경로와 활동이 시간 순서대로 기록됨.

AI 요약 카드: - "오늘의 난중일기" 섹션.

예시 텍스트: "2026년 10월 12일, 맑음. 오동도에서 동백꽃을 보고 진남관에서 역사를 배우다. 참으로 보람찬 하루였다." (생성형 AI가 쓴 듯한 문체)

NFT 배지: 획득한 여행 배지(뱃지)들을 그리드 형태로 나열.

4. 커뮤니티 (봉수대)

실시간 요청: "봉수 알림" 리스트. 관광객의 질문이나 도움 요청이 올라오는 피드.

참여하기: 각 요청에 대해 '답변하기' 또는 '출동하기' 버튼.

Data (Dummy Data)

User: 이름: "이순신(Guest)", 등급: "통제사", 포인트: 1,592냥

Missions: 1. 진남관 방문하기 (완료)
2. 여수당 쑥라떼 마시기 (진행중)
3. 해상 케이블카 탑승 (미완료)

Instructions

위 기능을 포함한 **단일 파일의 완성된 React 코드(App.js)**를 작성해주세요.

모바일 화면(폭 375~400px 기준)에서 가장 예쁘게 보이도록 레이아웃을 잡아주세요.

복잡한 백엔드 로직보다는 시각적 효과와 UI 인터랙션(탭 전환, 모달 팝업 등) 구현에 집중해주세요.

lucide-react 아이콘을 적절히 사용하여 직관적인 UI를 만들어주세요.

지금 바로 코드를 생성해주세요.

**Designing a Prototype App**

Okay, so I'm finally zeroing in on this "YE:ON" mobile web app. It's becoming clearer: a React SPA, TypeScript's my weapon, Tailwind for swift styling, Lucide-React for icons, and Framer Motion will provide the animations. The gamified Digital Navy tourism platform is the core!


**Visualizing UI Components**

I'm now diving into the UI details, starting with visual design. The Hopae card will have a wooden/gold appearance using gradients. Signal Kites require custom icons. The navigation will be a fixed bottom tab bar. I'm focusing on UI elements like cards, buttons, and glassmorphism. I'm thinking about the state management, but haven't chosen a solution yet.


**Structuring the App's Layout**

I'm now focusing on structure. I've designed the app with several key sections: Home, Map, Diary, and Community. I'm using `framer-motion` for transitions between tabs, and I'm designing specific components like `DigitalHopae`, `MissionMap`, `NanjungIlgi`, and `BongsuFeed`. I'm starting with core files like `index.tsx` and `App.tsx`, focusing on TypeScript and Tailwind CSS for styling. I've chosen colors, like `bg-slate-900` for the background and red/yellow for accents.


**Implementing the Design**

I'm now implementing the design in code. I'm prioritizing a professional look, ensuring responsiveness with a centered layout and max-width for desktop. I'm refining the "Hopae" card with a radial gradient or wood-like background. The "Bongsu-dae" icons will feature light/fire imagery. The structure is set with files like `metadata.json`, `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and component files. Now, I'll start coding.

Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS and fonts
React application entry point
Global TypeScript types and interfaces
Main application component with navigation and routing
Home screen with Digital Hopae card and dashboard

payload.text: 실제로 카메라와 연동해서 ar기능을 넣어줘, 그리고 모바일 화면 기준으로 앱을 만들어줘, 그리고 실제로 봉수를 작성해 올릴 수 있게 해줘

**Designing AR Integration**

I'm currently focused on integrating the AR functionality. The goal is to leverage the camera API to provide a live feed within the 'YE:ON' app. My design work centers around a mobile-first approach, with adjustments for the specific user context.


**Refining Mobile AR Design**

My focus has shifted to the mobile UX and AR design. I'm refining the AR experience, thinking about a `video` element with absolute positioning for AR elements. Community posting logic is next. I'll utilize `useState` to manage the list of alerts and "New Post" modal, as well as handling live camera feed integration with the camera API and incorporating post animations to provide an immediate community display to the user.


**Updating Component Architecture**

I'm now diving into the component architecture. Specifically, I'm refactoring the file structure to support live AR feed and community interaction. My current task is to modify `MapAR.tsx` to include the camera logic and `<video>` display. I'll also add a "Scanning" UI. Next up is `Community.tsx`, where I'm implementing state management for alerts and designing a posting modal that fits the "Beacon" theme.

entries.description: 실제 카메라 연동 AR 기능 구현 및 스캔 UI 개선
봉수대 작성 기능 추가 및 실시간 피드 업데이트 구현

payload.text: 카메라 화면 연동이 안됨, 허용을 했는데 화면은 그냥 검정색이야, 수정해줘 /  이순신(Guest) -> 정민서, 통제사 (명예 수군) -> 명예수군, Digital Navy ID -> 디지털 전라좌수영으로 바꿔줘

**Resolving AR Camera Glitch**

I'm currently working on resolving the black screen issue with the AR camera. Permission checks are confirmed, but the stream isn't rendering. I've also updated the UI text, swapping "이순신(Guest)" for "정민서" and "통제사 (명예 수군)" to an empty string. I'll need to double check the camera integration code.


**Integrating Camera Initialization Fixes**

I've been analyzing the camera initialization and integration. Specifically, the `MapView` component handles the camera functionality. I'm focusing on the `<video>` element within it and have identified potential fixes: ensuring `autoPlay`, `playsInline`, and `muted` are set, and confirming the `videoRef` is assigned before setting `srcObject`. I will also check initialization and delay to see if they might help. I will address a potential display issue related to the `Hopae` card on the home screen as well.


**Refining AR Stream Logic**

I'm now focusing on refining the `MapAR.tsx` component. I'm going to change the code so that it uses a `useEffect` hook to attach the stream only when `videoRef.current` is available and when `arMode` is true. This should fix the null reference error that's causing the black screen. I'll make sure to add the `muted` attribute to the video tag too. I will also add an `onCanPlay` event, to ensure the video can start. I will also check for the availability of `navigator.mediaDevices`.


**Implementing AR Mode Sequencing**

I'm now thinking about the order of operations in `startCamera`. I realize that setting `arMode` to `true` *before* attaching the stream to `videoRef.current` creates a race condition, leading to the null reference. My plan is to set `arMode` first, and then attach the stream in a `useEffect` hook that depends on both `arMode` and `videoRef`. I'm hopeful this synchronous sequencing will get things working.

entries.description: 카메라 검은 화면 문제 해결 (muted 속성 추가 및 비동기 스트림 연결 로직 개선)

payload.text: 봉수대 부분에 따로 글쓰는 부분이 위에 있으면 좋겠어 하단 아이콘있는거 별로야

**Relocating the UI**

I'm currently focused on the UI shift for the "Write Post" button. The primary change I'm making involves removing the floating action button (FAB) at the bottom within `Community.tsx`. The goal is to move its location.


**Positioning the Post Button**

I've decided to move the "Write" functionality to the top of the feed. I'm visualizing a card-like section prompting users to "Spread a new signal". This will replace the floating action button (FAB) at the bottom. I'm focusing on aesthetic consistency with a rounded button or input area, ideally in Deep Ocean Blue or Accent Red. I'm starting to modify `components/Community.tsx`.

entries.description: 봉수대 상단에 게시물 작성 버튼 추가 및 하단 플로팅 버튼 제거

payload.text:
**Examining UI Elements**

I'm currently reviewing the `App.tsx` file, specifically the header section. My focus is on identifying and isolating the UI element in the top left corner. I will then explore how to hide it.


**Isolating the "YE" Icon**

I've zeroed in on the code block responsible for the "YE" icon within the header. Specifically, I plan to remove the `div` element with the classes `w-8 h-8 bg-[#0F172A] rounded-lg`. This should address the user's explicit request based on their visual aid. I'm double-checking the other turn requests, and they remain in progress.


**Refining Code Changes**

I've made the necessary changes to the `App.tsx` file to remove the specified `div` element. The "YE" icon is no longer rendered, as requested. The UI now cleanly displays the "YE:ON" text, ensuring the requested change is complete. I'm satisfied with this targeted update.

entries.description: 좌측 상단 'YE' 로고 아이콘 제거

payload.text: 간단하게 ar 미션을 직접 해볼 수 있으면 좋겠어 클릭을 해서 연을 잡는다던가 ,,

**Developing Interactive AR**

I'm currently focused on implementing interactive logic within `components/MapAR.tsx` to enable catching the kite in the AR view. I'm adding state to track if the kite is caught. I'm exploring the best way to handle the click event and register that the kite has been "caught" by the user.


**Refining User Interactions**

I've been thinking about the user interactions, specifically the "Mission Accomplished" sequence. The goal is to provide a satisfying feedback loop when the user "catches" the kite. I'm focusing on making the kite clickable, and I'm planning to use CSS animations for the visual "catch" effect. I'm also considering a reward modal to reinforce the accomplishment.

entries.description: AR 미션에 인터랙티브 요소 추가: 화면에 떠다니는 연을 직접 클릭하여 '포획'하는 기능 구현

payload.text: ar 카메라 켜기는 필요 없을 듯

**Analyzing Button Removal**

Okay, I'm focusing on removing the "AR 카메라 켜기" button from the `MapView` component, which, as I see it, is located within `components/MapAR.tsx`. I've confirmed that the button exists at the top of this component, so, that's where I will start.


**Integrating AR Interaction**

I'm now considering the implications of removing the "AR 카메라 켜기" button. The user's feedback suggests a desire for a more streamlined AR experience. I am starting to think they'd prefer direct entry into the AR interaction, potentially bypassing the need for a separate activation step. It seems the "prototype" context leans towards showing the feature directly, so I will likely remove the floating button and its associated logic to create a smoother flow, and improve user testing.


**Simplifying AR Activation**

Okay, my focus now is on streamlining the AR activation process. I'm removing the "AR 미션 시작" button from the selected mission modal, and also the top floating "AR 카메라 켜기" button. The plan is to make clicking a mission marker *automatically* trigger the `startCamera` function. I am considering the user's intent. The prompt implies removing the button text. The user's past feedback about camera issues reinforces that they do want the camera.


**Eliminating Unnecessary Steps**

I've been going through the user's feedback, and now, my focus is on removing the top floating button and streamlining the mission flow in `components/MapAR.tsx`. I'm interpreting their "ar 카메라 켜기는 필요 없을 듯" as a clear instruction to eliminate that fixed, global button. My plan is to initiate the camera directly upon mission selection. I have already removed the logo, as per the earlier feedback.


**Refining AR Entry**

I am now focusing on refining the AR entry point based on user feedback. Removing the top button is confirmed. I am still keeping the button inside the mission popup for now. However, I am considering the user's desire for a more direct AR experience. This has led me to think about making the "Map" tab itself the gateway to AR, asking for camera permission immediately or at least providing a clear "Start" prompt.


**Streamlining Button Logic**

I am now eliminating the redundant "AR 카메라 켜기" button at the top of the map. I've reevaluated the user's intended flow: Map -> Marker -> Popup -> "AR 미션 시작" -> Camera. The top button was clearly unnecessary, given the mission-centric entry. I will focus on refining the mission popup. Additionally, I've considered that the AR view *is* the core experience, and the map may be secondary. I will update the code in `components/MapAR.tsx`.

entries.description: 상단 'AR 카메라 켜기' 버튼 제거 및 미션 선택 후 바로 AR 진입 가능하도록 UI 흐름 개선

payload.text: 이 어플리케이션 모바일 화면을 애플워치기준으로 워치 인터페이스로 전환 되는 버튼이 있어서 누르면 워치기준 프로토타입 앱을 볼 수 있으면 좋겠어 가능할까?

**Exploring Interface Options**

I've been digging into interface possibilities. My current focus is on a button toggle, enabling a seamless switch between the standard mobile view and an Apple Watch-style interface within the 'YE:ON' React app. I'm exploring the best placement and visual design for this toggle to ensure intuitive user interaction. This feature requires some thinking about UI elements that would be most common or useful in an Apple Watch type layout.


**Refining Watch UI Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the Apple Watch UI. I've integrated a device view state within `App.tsx` and am working on toggling between mobile and watch frames. The `WatchView` component is taking shape, and I'm simplifying the 4 core app sections. My goal is for the app to feel like a real smartwatch experience, with circular scrolling and minimalist, card-based displays. I plan to add the toggle in `HomeView`, near the existing sync button.


**Conceptualizing UI Designs**

I'm now fully immersed in developing both the mobile and Apple Watch UI. The mobile view remains as is, while the Apple Watch view now has definite design parameters: a centered, rounded screen with an aspect ratio of 184x224px. The visuals will mimic a smartwatch, potentially including digital crown and side button representations. The Digital Navy theme will be carried over for consistency. I will be adjusting layout management in `App.tsx` and adding logic to `Home.tsx` to switch views. The core functionality will be created in `WatchView.tsx`.

I will enhance the 'YE:ON' application by adding a high-fidelity **Apple Watch Interface Mode**. This will allow you to see how a "Digital Navy" member would interact with the service through their wearable device during their Yeosu mission.

### Specifications

1.  **View Switcher**: I'll transform the existing "Watch Sync" button on the Home screen into an interactive toggle. When clicked, it will trigger a sophisticated transition into the **Apple Watch Prototype View**.
2.  **Apple Watch Frame**: A realistic Apple Watch Ultra-inspired hardware frame will appear, featuring a rounded display, a digital crown, and an action button.
3.  **Watch OS Interface**:
    *   **Home (Complication Style)**: A glanceable view of the Digital Hopae (ID), current rank, and mission progress.
    *   **AR Compass (Map)**: A simplified navigation view showing the direction and distance to the nearest 'Signal Kite'.
    *   **Instant Bongsu (Alerts)**: Large, high-contrast buttons for quick emergency signaling or helpful reports.
    *   **Digital Crown Scrolling**: The watch content will be vertically scrollable with a smooth, haptic-style animation feel.
4.  **Visual Style**: Maintains the "Deep Ocean Blue" and "Obang-saek" accents, but optimized for OLED black backgrounds to maximize battery-saving aesthetics typical of smartwatches.

entries.description: Add view state to toggle between Mobile and Watch interfaces, and wrap the app in a device frame handler.
Add onToggleWatch prop to trigger the Watch view transition.

entries.description: Create the Apple Watch Interface component with realistic hardware frame and specialized wearOS UI.

payload.text: 워치 인터페이스에서 Navy ID -> 명예수군 으로 바꿔줘 그리고 인터페이스가 한 화면에 다 담기면 좋겠어

**Revising Apple Watch UI**

I'm focusing on the Apple Watch interface, specifically `components/WatchView.tsx`. My current focus is the label "Navy ID," which I'm changing to "명예수군" to reflect the user's rank. I'm also working towards a more compact display, aiming for a single-screen view without scrolling.


**Condensing the Watch Display**

I've been working on shrinking the display to fit within a single screen. My focus is still on `WatchView.tsx`, where I'm addressing excess padding and margins. The label "Navy ID" is now rendered as "명예수군", a direct replacement. Currently I'm reducing `pt-12` and other card paddings. I'm considering further adjustments like element size reduction.


**Fine-tuning WatchView Layout**

I'm making further layout adjustments to `WatchView.tsx`. The primary goal is denser information presentation. I'm scrutinizing card spacing, considering shrinking or eliminating the reward summary section and "End of Daily Report" spacer. Additionally, I'm working to reduce top padding for status bar spacing, hoping to tighten the overall layout significantly.

entries.description: 워치 인터페이스에서 'Navy ID'를 '명예수군'으로 변경하고, 한 화면에 모든 요소가 들어오도록 레이아웃 최적화
